title: Fixing a Node.js Express Middleware Route with Incorrect Route Resolution

statement.markdown: Implement a Node.js Express middleware to handle GET requests to a specific route. The route should be resolved based on a query parameter. However, the implementation has a logical bug that causes incorrect route resolution.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be a query string with a 'param' key
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the resolved route
statement.notes: The route resolution should be case-insensitive

editorial.title: Route Resolution
editorial.intuition: The route resolution should be case-insensitive
editorial.bruteForce: Check if the query parameter matches the route exactly
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a regular expression to match the route
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The route resolution should be case-insensitive
commonMistakes: Not using a case-insensitive string comparison

solutions.python:
from http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, HTTPServer

class Solution:
  def middleware(self, req, res, next):
    param = req.query.get('param')
    route = param.lower()
    res.writeHead(200, {'Content-Type': 'text/plain'})
    res.end(route)

server = HTTPServer(("localhost", 8000), Solution)
server.serve_forever()
